In charge Of dead engineer, an


Inland Empire electric train rush


ed into collision with another cleo


trio freight train at Freeman late
yesterday afternoon, according to


the theory of the company officials.


The engineer was o. w. Frost,


one of the best men on the line.


He was pulling second freight
and his train smashed into the one
ahead, which had stopped at the
station. Floyd Marshall, a brake
man, had gone back to flag the
train, but claims he could not at
tract the attention OF Frost From


this the officials argue that the en-
gineer had died of apoplexy.
Coroner Witter, who examined


the mangled body. says it was i'm
possible to tell whether death had
resulted previous TO or as a result
of the collision, as the injuries sus-
tanned would have been fatal in
any event.


The electric locomotive was bad


ly damaged by the collision and a
couple of cars in the train ahead
were also splintered.


Electric railroad men say that if


Frosts hand had left the controll-


err the current would have been
shut off automatically, so that if


the apoplexy theory is correct he


must have retained grip on the
lever.
